title: Docker Buildx CI, Tests, Context, Cache, and Multi-Platform Output · Run and Artifact Forensics
summary: A release responder inherits a plausible but defective container build and test workflow; preserve run evidence, isolate the first causal fault in docker buildx ci, tests, context, cache, and multi-platform output, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in container build and test workflow and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Configure official Docker setup and build actions with explicit Git or path context, Dockerfile, target, platforms, inputs, and output mode.
Use secret mounts, scoped cache backends, deterministic keys, trusted writers, and no credential-bearing ARG, layer, log, or attestation.
Test image configuration, non-root and read-only behavior, health, signals, resources, network, changed config, and failure before registry mutation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Build Record Summary Debug
steps.instruction:
Order the Ci Build Record Summary Debug delivery-system evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Build Record Summary Debug.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ci Build Record Summary Debug assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Build Record Summary Debug, keep Ci Build Record Summary Debug connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: A debug Buildx workflow leaks a secret through ARG, trusts a fork cache, builds only amd64, pushes before tests, and cannot tie output to source. Separate builder, context, platforms, cache, test, secret, and digest evidence. Competency probe: Retain build record, summary, stages, timings, cache use, inputs, warnings, digest, and controlled reproduction instructions without secrets.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Build Record Summary Debug
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-build-record-summary-debug-1sezu7". Tailor its container-build-workflow.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Build Record Summary Debug.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Build Record Summary Debug into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Build Record Summary Debug, keep Ci Build Record Summary Debug connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-build-record-summary-debug-1sezu7
  - competency: ci-build-record-summary-debug
    artifact: container-build-workflow.yaml
    decision: build record summary debug
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: make the service container start before it is ready
    retained-proof: OIDC claims provider condition service account IAM and audit entry
    case-key: 1sezu7
    change: |
      setup-buildx-action: 4
      build-push-action: 7
      context: explicit-path-after-generation
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      platforms: [linux-amd64, linux-arm64]
      secret: buildkit-secret-mount
      cache-writer: trusted-main-only
      push: after-platform-tests
      build-record: retained
      output-digest: sha256-verified
    verify: render build inputs inspect build record test each platform changed config signals health and read-only behavior then verify index digest
    failure: Reject implicit Git context, ARG secrets, fork-writable cache, build-only proof, one-platform tests, or push-before-validation.
    repair: Select context deliberately, mount secrets, scope cache, test runtime behavior per platform, retain records, and push verified output.
